name: Partial Reprogramming Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Partial Reprogramming need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair staged source, study, finding, registry-watch, review, safety-boundary, seminal-anchor, support-map, and coverage-assessment records for Partial Reprogramming. The repair adds cyclic OSKM, retinal OSK, long-term OSKM, AAV OSK, Cdkn2a-OSK, full-reprogramming teratoma-boundary, model-organism limiting, 2026 review, and ER-100 phase 1 no-results registry records. The staged outlook keeps the track at low-confidence animal_signal with accelerating momentum and updates the public human boundary to no human outcomes evidence rather than no human program.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair materially changes the public support map and boundary language without changing the stage: animal anchors are now auditable, safety and taxonomy limits are explicit, recent-review triangulation is represented, and the ER-100 registry is visible as no-results trial-watch context rather than evidence of benefit.

search_log.query: partial reprogramming aging OSK OR OSKM Yamanaka aging; partial cellular reprogramming rejuvenation review; selected PMIDs 27984723, 33268865, 37118377, 38381405, 39259812, 24025773, 39267613, 41490578
search_log.summary: Confirmed metadata and abstracts for core animal anchors, tissue-specific OSK function, long-term OSKM molecular aging effects, 2024 AAV and targeted OSK papers, a full-reprogramming teratoma safety boundary, a model-organism null/negative result, and a 2026 review emphasizing schedule, delivery, and safety pharmacology.

search_log.query: "partial reprogramming"; "Yamanaka factors"; "Oct4 Sox2 Klf4"; "OSK" "aging"; "epigenetic reprogramming" "aging"; "cellular reprogramming" "aging"; "epigenetic rejuvenation"; "rejuvenation" "Yamanaka"
search_log.summary: Exact partial-reprogramming and Yamanaka-factor searches returned no studies, but broader OSK/aging and epigenetic-reprogramming/aging searches identified NCT07290244, a recruiting Life Biosciences ER-100 phase 1 ocular AAV OSK safety study with no posted results.

search_log.source_name: Local public records and prior review artifacts
search_log.query: Partial Reprogramming outlook, May 30 surveillance revision bundle, evidence reviews, publication event, June 12 coverage assessment, local source/finding searches, and planning state
search_log.summary: Confirmed the previous public outlook was appropriately conservative but lacked curated source, study, finding, review, safety, registry, and taxonomy-boundary records. The new repair stages those records without publishing them.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged as a recruiting phase 1 no-results ER-100 AAV OSK ocular safety registry. Month-level registry fields list primary completion as May 2027 and study completion as March 2032.

excluded_sources.reason: Exact searches for "partial reprogramming", "Yamanaka factors", and "Oct4 Sox2 Klf4" returned no ClinicalTrials.gov studies in the June 12, 2026 API checks; the direct human registry was found through broader OSK/aging and epigenetic-reprogramming/aging searches.

excluded_sources.title: Exercise-Induced Epigenetic Modifications in Obese Aging Women
excluded_sources.reason: Exercise and diet epigenetic-modification study in metabolic syndrome, not partial reprogramming or OSK/Yamanaka-factor therapy.

excluded_sources.title: The Use of Aging Biomarkers in Saliva, Based on DNA Methylation Status in Elovl2 to Predict Biological Age
excluded_sources.reason: Biomarker-prediction study, not an interventional partial-reprogramming therapy.

excluded_sources.title: Cellular rejuvenation protects neurons from inflammation-mediated cell death
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant disease-context OSK neuroprotection paper, but the repair prioritized aging-track animal lifespan, tissue-function, safety, review, and current human registry anchors.

excluded_sources.title: Conserved biological processes in partial cellular reprogramming: Relevance to aging and rejuvenation
excluded_sources.reason: Useful review context, but the staged 2026 review more directly addressed current translation, schedule, delivery, and safety-pharmacology gaps for this bounded repair.

notes: The staged repair intentionally keeps evidence_stage at animal_signal because no human partial-reprogramming results are posted or published.
The ER-100 registry is represented as regulatory/program activity with inconclusive direction, not as safety or efficacy evidence.
Month-only ClinicalTrials.gov completion dates were preserved in notes rather than normalized into exact structured dates.
The bundle stages public record candidates only and does not approve or publish them.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, taxonomy-mapping, and safety-limitations review for the staged Partial Reprogramming coverage-repair bundle.

If approved and published, return Partial Reprogramming to ordinary surveillance with watch on NCT07290244 result postings, ER-100 program updates, and independent replication or limitation of the 2024 mouse lifespan and safety signals.
